title: Refine the frontend file map for session hotspots
summary: Sharpened the file map into a hotspot view that highlights repeated TSX and JSX touch points across sessions and makes repeated frontend pressure visible.
goal: Use file-level aggregation to show where the frontend work is clustering so teams can spot fragile areas before collisions happen.
changesMade: Reworked file map ordering around session count and touch density
Tagged repeated hotspots with stronger visual emphasis
Kept per-session contribution badges compact enough for dense maps
Made file path presentation friendlier for mixed JSX and TSX areas

features.name: hotspot-first file map
features.why: Operators need to spot overloaded frontend files quickly when many sessions converge on the same components.

features.name: mixed JSX and TSX file labeling
features.why: The demo corpus includes both React todo JSX and Next dashboard TSX work.

features.name: aggregation cleanup
features.why: The file map should stay legible even when twenty or more sessions exist in the repo.

decisions.decision: Sort hotspots by breadth before raw touch count
decisions.why: A file touched by many sessions is often riskier than a file hammered by one session.

decisions.decision: Keep the file map inside the main dashboard tabs
decisions.why: Hotspot review is part of regular session triage, not a separate admin mode.

openQuestions: Should the file map collapse same-directory hotspots into groups once the list gets long?
resumePrompt: Continue the hotspot map by stress-testing dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx against wider session data and then trim any noisy labeling in dashboard/src/lib/data.ts.
